examples.title: Pseudocode: Mutual Exclusion
examples.description: Demonstrates how a binary semaphore ensures mutual exclusion between multiple processes.
examples.explanation: The binary semaphore mutex (initialized to 1) ensures that only one process can be in its critical section at a time.

examples.title: Python: Producer-Consumer Problem
examples.description: Solves the Producer-Consumer problem using counting semaphores.
examples.code:
import threading
import time

# Shared variables
buffer = [None] * 5
mutex = threading.Semaphore(1)
empty = threading.Semaphore(5)
full = threading.Semaphore(0)

# Producer function
def producer():
 for i in range(10):
 time.sleep(1)
 empty.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 buffer[i % 5] = i
 mutex.release()
 full.release()

# Consumer function
def consumer():
 for i in range(10):
 time.sleep(2)
 full.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 print(buffer[i % 5])
 mutex.release()
 empty.release()

# Create threads
producer_thread = threading.Thread(target=producer)
consumer_thread = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

# Start threads
producer_thread.start()
consumer_thread.start()

# Wait for threads to finish
producer_thread.join()
consumer_thread.join()
examples.explanation: The producer-consumer problem is solved using counting semaphores empty and full to manage the buffer, and a binary semaphore mutex for mutual exclusion.

examples.title: C: Binary Semaphore
examples.description: Demonstrates how a binary semaphore can be used for mutual exclusion in C.
examples.explanation: The binary semaphore mutex is used to ensure mutual exclusion between the main thread and the created thread.
